skill.name: image-to-video
skill.description: Routes still-image animation requests to RunComfy image-to-video models, including HappyHorse, Wan lip-sync, and Seedance multimodal generation.
skill.summary: Animate still images with the right RunComfy video model for each request.
skill.author: agentspace-so

security_audit.summary: The static analyzer reported many command, network, filesystem, and blocker patterns. Review found no malicious intent or prompt injection, but the skill intentionally invokes the RunComfy CLI, sends user media references to RunComfy services, and writes generated files locally. Most high-severity static alerts were markdown, placeholder URL, or model-name false positives.

medium_findings.title: Third-Party CLI Execution
medium_findings.description: The skill instructs users to install and run the RunComfy CLI. This is legitimate for the skill purpose, but it executes local commands and requires trust in the CLI package and account authentication.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The command examples and explanation directly show local RunComfy CLI execution. The usage is intentional and documented, with no evidence of shell expansion of user prompts.

medium_findings.title: Outbound Media Processing Through RunComfy
medium_findings.description: The skill sends prompts and media URLs to RunComfy model APIs and downloads generated outputs. Users should treat source media, prompts, and generated results as data shared with a third-party service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The skill explicitly describes API submission, polling, remote media fetching, and download endpoints. This confirms network transfer, but it matches the advertised video generation behavior.

medium_findings.title: Local Token and Output File Handling
medium_findings.description: The skill documents local token storage and output directory writes. These are normal CLI behaviors, but they affect credential handling and local disk usage.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file paths and token storage are documented directly. The token location uses owner-only permissions, so the risk is operational rather than evidence of malicious file access.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal Alerts Are Placeholder URL False Positives
low_findings.description: The analyzer flagged path traversal on example values that use abbreviated HTTPS URLs. These are documentation placeholders for media inputs, not local relative path traversal.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The lines are inside fenced command examples and contain HTTPS placeholder media URLs. No local file path concatenation or traversal sink is present in the reviewed file.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Reconnaissance Alerts Are False Positives
low_findings.description: The blocker alerts appear to match markdown terms, prompt fields, model notes, or exit-code documentation. No cryptographic implementation or system reconnaissance command is present.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited lines describe YAML front matter, model prompt fields, negative prompts, and documentation headings. I found no hashing, encryption, host probing, or system inventory behavior.

dangerous_patterns.title: Documented CLI Command Execution
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill uses command examples that install or invoke a third-party CLI. Users should review the CLI package and avoid passing untrusted shell-expanded input outside the documented JSON argument pattern.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The commands are visible in fenced bash examples. They are not hidden automation, but they still rely on local command execution.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential File and Environment Token Use
dangerous_patterns.description: The skill references a local token file and an environment token option. These are expected authentication mechanisms, but they require normal secret handling controls.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The token storage behavior is explicitly documented. The file permission note reduces severity, but token exposure would still affect account security.

content.value_statement: Teams often waste time choosing the wrong image-to-video model for a creative request. This skill routes each request to a suitable RunComfy model and supplies model-specific prompting guidance.
actual_capabilities: Routes general image animation requests to HappyHorse 1.0 I2V.
Routes custom voiceover lip-sync requests to Wan 2.7 with audio_url.
Routes multimodal image, video, and audio reference requests to Seedance 2.0 Pro.
Provides documented RunComfy CLI invocation patterns for each route.
Summarizes model limits for duration, resolution, aspect ratio, and input formats.
Includes prompting tips for motion, camera movement, identity preservation, and reference media.
limitations: Requires the RunComfy CLI, a RunComfy account, and authentication before use.
Depends on remote RunComfy model availability, quotas, and service behavior.
Uses one model route per call and does not stitch outputs together.
Requires source media URLs that satisfy each selected model input limit.

use_cases.title: Create Product Motion Clips
use_cases.description: Animate a product image with a controlled camera move while preserving packaging, geometry, and brand details.

use_cases.title: Produce Localized Spokesperson Videos
use_cases.description: Generate multiple talking-head variants from one concept by changing the voiceover URL and language.

use_cases.title: Build Reference-Guided Video Concepts
use_cases.description: Combine a subject image, scene reference video, and audio reference into one short generated clip.

prompt_templates.title: Animate a Single Image
prompt_templates.scenario: Beginner request for a simple image-to-video clip.
prompt_templates.prompt: Turn this image URL into a short video. Use smooth camera motion, preserve the subject identity, and choose the best default RunComfy route.

prompt_templates.title: Create a Product Reveal
prompt_templates.scenario: Intermediate request that needs stable product details.
prompt_templates.prompt: Animate this product image as a five-second reveal. Keep packaging text stable, use a slow orbit, and avoid warped geometry.

prompt_templates.title: Generate a Lip-Synced Voiceover Clip
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request with a custom audio URL.
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a vertical talking-head clip using this voiceover URL. Match the duration to the audio and avoid subtitles or flicker.

prompt_templates.title: Compose a Multimodal Reference Video
prompt_templates.scenario: Advanced request using image, video, and audio references.
prompt_templates.prompt: Use image 1 for the subject, video 1 for scene motion, and audio 1 for voice tone. Generate an eight-second narrative shot.

output_examples.input: A portrait image needs subtle motion and identity stability.
output_examples.output: The skill selects HappyHorse 1.0 I2V and prepares a prompt focused on breathing, blinking, soft light, and stable facial features.

output_examples.input: A product photo needs a short social video with a slow camera orbit.
output_examples.output: The skill routes to HappyHorse, keeps the original aspect ratio, and emphasizes unchanged packaging and smooth camera movement.

output_examples.input: A spokesperson clip needs a custom English voiceover and vertical format.
output_examples.output: The skill selects Wan 2.7, uses the voiceover URL for lip-sync, and sets a vertical talking-head framing prompt.

best_practices: Use direct HTTPS media URLs that meet the selected model size and duration limits.
Describe motion, camera movement, lighting changes, and preservation goals clearly.
Keep one primary motion beat per clip for more stable short videos.
anti_patterns: Do not mix unrelated visual styles across references unless drift is acceptable.
Do not expect one route to combine HappyHorse animation and Wan lip-sync in a single call.
Do not pass sensitive media or prompts unless third-party RunComfy processing is acceptable.

faq.question: Which model does the skill choose by default?
faq.answer: It defaults to HappyHorse 1.0 I2V for general image animation, portraits, products, and native audio requests.

faq.question: Can it lip-sync to my own audio?
faq.answer: Yes. It routes custom voiceover requests to Wan 2.7 with an audio_url input.

faq.question: Can it use reference video and audio together?
faq.answer: Yes. It routes multimodal reference requests to Seedance 2.0 Pro.

faq.question: Does it run without a RunComfy account?
faq.answer: No. The skill requires the RunComfy CLI and either login or a RUNCOMFY_TOKEN value.

faq.question: Does the skill send data to a third-party service?
faq.answer: Yes. The RunComfy CLI sends prompts and media references to RunComfy model APIs for processing.

faq.question: Can it generate long videos?
faq.answer: No. The documented routes are intended for short clips, with model-specific limits up to 15 seconds.
